screens.name: Theme Usage Protocol
screens.purpose: Make the selected design profile a required reference for web changes without encoding the theme as global CSS variables.
screens.primary_action: Read and record the chosen profile/register before changing rendered web UI.
states: runtime CSS unchanged unless explicitly approved

screens.name: Songs Pages
screens.purpose: Keep songs list and detail pages rendering after removing custom CSS theme variables.

screens.name: Interval Quiz Page
screens.purpose: Keep quiz interaction rendering after removing custom CSS theme variables.
screens.primary_action: Reveal answer or advance to next question.

ux_rules: Web work must select the applicable profile/register from aesthetic-profile.json before visual edits.
Run artifacts must record the selected profile/register and any deviations.
Do not use globals.css as a repository-specific theme dump; keep it limited to Tailwind/shadcn runtime variables and base CSS.
Use shadcn/Tailwind semantic utilities for rendered surfaces unless a task explicitly approves a new runtime token layer.
Browser verification must compare before/after pages when removing styling infrastructure.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Move theme decisions into workflow and review artifacts instead of global CSS variables.
interaction_risks: Agents may skip profile selection if the protocol is not listed in required reads.
Future changes may reintroduce ad-hoc CSS variables unless runtime-token rules are explicit.
notes: This asset has no rendered UI surface.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Use existing page structure while replacing custom CSS-variable styling with semantic Tailwind/shadcn utilities.
interaction_risks: Color contrast may change after falling back to semantic tokens.
Lead sheet annotations need to remain legible after text color class migration.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep a focused quiz card using semantic backgrounds, borders, foreground, and primary action classes.
interaction_risks: Primary button contrast and focus ring must remain clear.
Loading and answer states must remain readable.

constraints: Create theme-usage.md and link it from design-rule-engine README and website workflow.
Update agent prompts so future web work reads theme-usage.md.
Do not install external design skills.
open_decisions: Whether future tasks introduce a dedicated token system remains open.

constraints: Remove references to --tone-*, --space-*, and --font-body from TSX.
Remove type-* class usage from TSX.
Prefer bg-background, bg-card, bg-muted, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border, ring-ring, and bg-primary/text-primary-foreground.
open_decisions: Exact future visual tone should be decided by a separate profile application task.

constraints: Keep the existing quiz behavior unchanged.
Preserve visible loading, hidden answer, and visible answer states.
Use semantic button and focus classes rather than custom accent variables.

critical_judgments: Future web work is required to consult aesthetic-profile.json and theme-usage.md.
Theme decisions are recorded in artifacts, not hidden in CSS variables.
must_not_regress: No external skill is claimed as installed.
No workflow bypasses browser verification for visual UI edits.

critical_judgments: The songs list and detail pages load without error overlays.
The lead sheet remains legible and fits available width.
must_not_regress: Representative lead sheet rows do not render blank or overlap incoherently.

critical_judgments: The quiz page loads without error overlays.
The primary action remains visually obvious and keyboard-focusable.
must_not_regress: Question and answer text remain readable.

visual_constraints: Before screenshots are required for /songs, a representative song detail page, and /quiz before CSS migration.
After screenshots are required for the same routes.
Served CSS must be checked for absence of removed custom variables and type selectors.
